2026-04-01T08:25:10.0573349Z Agent: 2. `Request_2` and `Response_2` - these have `_N` suffixes due to collision with built-in types (Request and Response are global types). However, looking more carefully, these are internal types that have `export { Request_2 as Request }` so they're exported as `Request` and `Response`. The `_2` suffix is just an internal alias name to avoid collision. 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0575458Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0577962Z Agent: Now I need to check the guidelines to see how these should be flagged. 2026-04-01T08:25:10.0586527Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0587139Z Agent: 1. **`apiRefLink` with `?view=azure-node-preview`** (line 571 in package.json) - This is a stable release (19.0.0), so using `?view=azure-node-preview` is a tool issue.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0587818Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0588598Z Agent: 2. **`AutoGeneratedDomainNameLabelScope`** (line 957 in arm-appservice-node.api.md) - This has an "AutoGenerated" prefix in the type name. But wait, the guideline says "Avoid `AutoGenerated` suffixes" not prefix. 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0589445Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0592835Z Agent: Looking back at the guideline more carefully, it specifically mentions avoiding `AutoGenerated` suffixes like when both `Resource` and `ResourceAutoGenerated` exist. In this case, `AutoGeneratedDomainNameLabelScope` has "AutoGenerated" as a prefix rather than a suffix, which doesn't match the pattern described in the guidelines, so this might not actually be a violation. The guideline only applies when there's a naming collision between two types, so `AutoGeneratedDomainNameLabelScope` standing alone isn't a violation—it's just reflecting the actual spec property name where "AutoGenerated" carries semantic meaning about how Azure generates the domain name. Now I need to look more closely at the `Request_2` and `Response_2` naming issue.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0595632Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0597354Z Agent: These have `_2` suffixes, which the guidelines discourage, but the real problem here is that `Request_2` is being exported as `Request`—this creates a collision with the well-known DOM `Request` type, which is exactly what the guidelines warn against. The `_2` suffix is just an internal workaround to avoid that collision, but the underlying issue is that we're exporting a name that clashes with a global web type.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0598750Z 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0599343Z ✓ $ grep -n -A 10 "^interface Request_2" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/sdk/appservice/arm-appservice/review/arm-appservice-node.api.md
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0599916Z 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0600488Z ✓ $ grep -n -A 10 "^interface Response_2" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/sdk/appservice/arm-appservice/review/arm-appservice-node.api.md
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0601341Z 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0601429Z Agent: Looking at this:
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0601992Z Agent: - `Request_2` is an Azure API type (request headers/method/uri), exported as `Request` - this conflicts with the global DOM `Request` type.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0603430Z Agent: - `Response_2` is an Azure API type (body/headers/statusCode), exported as `Response` - this conflicts with the global DOM `Response` type.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0604030Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0604224Z Agent: According to the guidelines:
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0604891Z Agent: > Avoid exporting names that clash with well-known web/DOM types (e.g. `Request`, `Response`, `Event`). Use a service-specific prefix when collisions are likely.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0605557Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0605963Z Agent: This is a violation. These should be renamed to something like `HttpRequest` or `WorkflowRequest` etc.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0606460Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0606621Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0607989Z Agent: The `_2` suffix indicates the TypeScript compiler encountered a naming collision and auto-renamed it, which is exactly the pattern the guidelines warn against. Since this is a new TypeSpec-generated package (migrated from Swagger in 18.0.0), the whole package was regenerated, though these naming issues likely originate from the spec itself.
2026-04-01T08:25:10.0609261Z Agent:

Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added. Target: 37912.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. 2026-04-01T08:25:10.3088413Z       }
2026-04-01T08:25:10.3088487Z     ]
2026-04-01T08:25:10.3088556Z   }
2026-04-01T08:25:10.3088629Z }
